Description:
Take the lead in your own learning. Use your half days to explore topics that matter to you. Focus on activities that support your personal development, build new skills, and enhance your confidence in your role.

Self‑directed learning might include:
Choosing online modules relevant to your role
Reading articles, research, or guidance
Watching recorded webinars
Using half‑day learning time for a personal project
Reflecting on how new learning can improve practice

In today's fast-paced and demanding environments, stress can quickly become overwhelming—especially when support feels limited or out of reach. This session focuses on practical ways to move from stress and pressure toward greater support, resilience, and connection.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: This session aims to raise awareness and looks at some of the main health conditions which may affect men in their lifetime. There is a focus on both physical and mental health with some practical tools to help you stay focused on your health and wellbeing.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: This session focuses on blood pressure and cholesterol and helps you to 'know your numbers'. We demonstrate pratical tips to support your cardiovascular health and promote a healthy lifestyle to manage or improve your health and wellbeing.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: This practical sessions looks at the contributing factors to aches and pains that women may experience during the Menopause. We also look at how to manage these symptoms through self-care with a focus on eating and hydrating well, movement and stress management.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: This session focuses on the importance of YOU and your Health and Wellbeing. We demonstrate how to make self-care habitual by using practical tips to support your overall health and wellbeing. The session covers the 4 key areas of wellbeing with tips on how to: Eat and Hydrate Well, Sleep Well, Think Well and Feel/Move Well.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: This Toolbox Talk teaches you about the importance of Hydration and the link to your Health, Safety and Wellbeing at work. You will leave armed with information about how much to drink, how to do self-checks to ensure you are adequately hydrated and tips on encouraging you to stay focused on hydration.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: In this session you will learn practical tools to create spaces where people feel safe, respected, and heard. We will look at how to foster psychological safety, encourage open dialogue, and navigate challenging or sensitive topics with confidence. Ideal for facilitators, leaders, educators, and anyone guiding group conversations who wants to support inclusive, respectful, and meaningful dialogue.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: During this interactive session staff will learn practical tips on how to promote healthy sleep habits and bring on a state of relaxation easily to aid restorative sleep.
Trainer: Health and Wellbeing Team deivered by HALSA Wellbeing

Description: This session explores the 'Active Bystander' concept, providing practical tools to safely challenge inappropriate behaviors and foster a more inclusive and supportive environment.
An informative session focused on understanding reasonable adjustments in the workplace, ensuring equity for staff with disabilities or long-term health conditions.
This awareness session covers the importance of the Rainbow Badge scheme, LGBTQ+ inclusivity, and how to be an effective ally within the organization.
A deep dive into cultural humility, moving beyond competence to a lifelong commitment to self-evaluation and critique to better support a diverse workforce and community.

Data Saves Lives - Why Does Data Matter To Me?
Description: Offered as part of the Trust’s bi-monthly Data Quality programme, this interactive session explores how accurate data underpins patient safety, care quality, performance, and funding. Using real Trust examples and practical exercises, staff will learn the principles of a high-quality patient record, identify common data entry errors in SystmOne, and understand how small mistakes can have significant clinical, operational, and financial impacts.  The session reinforces that data quality is everyone’s responsibility, regardless of clinical or non‑clinical role.
Trainer: Information Quality Manager / Senior Information Quality Officer

Description: This session provides an overview of the new analytic dashboards on Radar
TargetAudience: Managers and colleagues who have oversight of incidents and audits

Description:
Keeping your retirement plans on track can be a
challenging task. The retirement course encourages
a positive and realistic approach to help you make
informed choices about your retirement plans.

SYEDA are pleased to launch the first in a series of e-learning modules focused on Neurodivergence and Eating Disorders, which focuses on Avoidant/Restrictive Food Intake Disorder (ARFID). This module is split into 2 parts:
 
•	Understanding ARFID
•	Treatment and practically supporting someone with ARFID.
 
To access this module, you will first need to sign up to SYEDA's learning platform (Log in to the site | LMS) and then enrol in the module.

I’m Kate, a mindfulness and meditation teacher working within the Moving Forward service at Tickhill Road Hospital, and each month I host a relaxed and welcoming health and wellbeing podcast for RDASH staff. In each episode, I’m joined by a different member of staff and together we have an informal, down-to-earth chat about wellbeing in all its forms. The conversations are designed to be relatable and engaging, with a blend of gentle advice, practical tips, inspiration, motivation, and a touch of humour. It’s a space to pause, reflect, and connect—supporting staff to look after their wellbeing in a way that feels realistic, accessible, and human.
An introductory session to Avoidant Restrictive Food Intake Disorder

The Poverty Proofing training is for colleagues working in Rotherham CAMHS. This session relates directly to the Poverty Proofing audit which is currently taking place within the service. Colleagues are encouraged to attend to find out about the programme, Promise 6, how poverty impacts our patients and to share thoughts/experiences
TargetAudience:
LHD for colleagues in Rotherham CAMHS service:    • Getting Help  
• Getting Advice 
• Mental Health Support Team  
• Psychological Therapies 
• Children in Care 
• Intellectual Disabilities

Course: speak up listen up follow up
Description:
Speak up- This module covers what speaking up is and why it matters. It will help you understand how you can speak up and what to expect. Listen up- This module focuses on listening to concerns and understanding the barriers to speaking up. Managers should complete both Speak Up and Listen Up to ensure they understand what speaking up is and how they should respond when someone speaks up to them. follow up- The final module – Follow Up – completes the package. Developed for senior leaders throughout healthcare – including executive and non-executive directors, lay members and governors – this module aims to promote a consistent and effective Freedom to Speak Up culture across the system which enables workers to speak up and be confident they will be listened to and action taken.
Senior leaders are expected to complete all three modules, Speak Up, Listen Up and Follow Up to ensure they have a full understanding of the speaking up process.
Trainer: This session is provided externally via NHSE

Description: Freedom to Speak Up (FTSU) training is designed to help all staff feel confident to raise concerns about patient safety, quality, culture, or inappropriate behaviour at work. The training explains what speaking up means, why it matters, and how the Freedom to Speak Up Guardian can support you to raise issues safely and confidentially. It also outlines the different ways you can speak up, what to expect after you do so, and how the organisation is committed to listening, learning, and acting on concerns, with no detriment to those who raise them. This training supports a safe, open, and supportive workplace where everyone’s voice is valued.
Come for a chat about menopause, led by the attendees - it doesn't matter what stage of menopause you're at, your gender, or if it's someone you support that's going through it
With our Macmillan info team and PFG to promote the work and value of Peer support workers
